issues.title: enforcementCheck() reads wrong JSON property for coverage threshold
issues.description: Line 116: enforce.required_checks.coverage_minimum does not exist in APPFINISHER_ENFORCEMENT.json. Correct path is enforce.thresholds.minimum_coverage_percent. Bug caused coverage enforcement to silently always pass (coverage < undefined evaluates to false in JS).
issues.notes: This was a silent failure — the coverage check always passed regardless of actual coverage because undefined comparisons evaluate to false. Critical enforcement gap.

issues.title: main() called unconditionally at module bottom — prevents testability
issues.description: The module called main() at the bottom without an ESM main guard. Any import of runner.js would trigger main() → validateSetup() → fail() if appfinisher/ dir not found. This made the module impossible to import in tests without crashing.
issues.fix_applied: Added 'import { fileURLToPath } from url', defined __filename constant, replaced main() call with ESM main guard: if (process.argv[1] && path.resolve(process.argv[1]) === __filename) { main(); }. Added exports for all functions.
issues.notes: Standard ESM entry-point pattern. Also added exports to enable unit testing of individual functions.
